instructions: You are the routing orchestrator. For each row, derive the pattern card(s) it activates, the strength (strong|weak|negative), whether it is a held defense, and — only for reflection/echo/render rows — the default verdict you would assign WITHOUT execution/canary proof. You do NOT have verdicts, strengths, or expected mappings. Output the contract in evals/qualification/orchestrator-brief.md. Use the row ids verbatim.

rows.observation: a chat-read request carries an owner-id field in the query/body that differs from the authenticated session principal
two versions of the same chat endpoint (v1 and v2) are both mounted and answering
the newer route version calls an owner-access validation helper that the older version does not
there is a UI model picker, but the chosen model id also travels in the request body
the client sends a mandatory-tools list in the request body
model output is rendered through a markdown / diagram pipeline
a server-side model filter is applied to every request regardless of user class
export/owner resolution is taken from the authenticated principal, not from a request field
